interactions.summary: Jiang develops the Russia-China alliance layer through classroom prompts: Russia benefits if the United States is distracted from Ukraine, China needs Middle Eastern oil, and Iran must prove it can fight and win before they invest more heavily.

Jiang explains global opinion and enemy weakening: Gaza increases sympathy toward Iran, domestic dissent can fracture a U.S.-led coalition, and Operation True Promise is presented as a concrete application of all four matrix goals.
Jiang asks who stopped Israel from responding disproportionately and sets up the answer that the United States restrained Israel.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang with a short uncaptured student response

speaker_notes.note: The packet includes multiple classroom prompts and 'yes' or 'exactly' acknowledgements; the substantive content is Jiang's restatement and answer.

claims.claim: Jiang argues Iran needs a second alliance layer with Russia and China if it is to win a war against the United States and Israel.
Jiang says Russia would likely not deploy soldiers for Iran but could limit U.S. options by warning against the use of tactical nuclear weapons.
claims.temporal_scope: Conditional prediction about a future Iran war, stated on 2024-04-24.

claims.claim: Jiang says China has an interest in preventing U.S. control over Iraq and Iran because China depends heavily on Middle Eastern oil.
Jiang says Iran must prove to Russia and China that it will fight and can win before they invest more financially, militarily, and politically.
Jiang argues Iran needs world attention to remain on Gaza because outrage over Gaza will make global opinion more sympathetic toward Iran over time.
Jiang describes Israel's Gaza policy as a campaign to remove Palestinians from Gaza, and calls what is happening in Gaza basically genocide.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's characterization of events as of 2024-04-24.

claims.claim: Jiang says Iran can weaken the enemy by increasing internal dissent in the United States and creating conflict between NATO and the United States over a possible invasion of Iran.

Jiang argues Operation True Promise was designed to satisfy all four matrix goals: show Iranians that Iran can strike back, signal willingness to fight to allies and major powers, preserve global opinion by avoiding casualties, and provoke tension between Israel and the United States.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretation of Operation True Promise stated on 2024-04-24.

claims.claim: Jiang defines Israel's disproportionality policy as using overwhelming retaliation to produce deterrence and make enemies afraid to attack Israel.

signature_moments.moment: Iran has to convince Russia and China like an investor pitch: we will fight, and we will win.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Why will I invest in you, Iran?... we will fight. Second thing is, we will win
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This compresses alliance-building into Jiang's broader logic of credibility, investment, and proof under asymmetric pressure.

signature_moments.moment: Global opinion turns on keeping Gaza in the world's spotlight.
signature_moments.source_phrase: It must maintain the world spotlight on Gaza
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Jiang makes global sympathy a strategic resource and connects Iran's restraint to the optics of Gaza.

signature_moments.moment: Disproportionality is deterrence by arithmetic: one Israeli becomes a hundred or a thousand dead enemies.
signature_moments.source_phrase: if you kill one Israeli, they'll come and kill about a hundred of you, a thousand of you
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The phrasing captures Jiang's stark model of Israeli deterrence and why U.S. restraint matters in his argument.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A strategic audience Jiang treats as necessary for Iran to win; restraint and the Gaza spotlight help Iran gain sympathy.
Israel's deterrent practice, as Jiang describes it, of responding to limited attacks with overwhelming force.
Making opponents afraid enough of retaliation that they do not dare attack.

chronology_notes.note: The Russia-China, Gaza, and Operation True Promise readings are tied to Jiang's 2024-04-24 assessment of the April 2024 confrontation.

uncertainty_notes.note: Several student answers are implied by Jiang's prompts and acknowledgements but not captured verbatim in the transcript.
